_meta.name: tux
_meta.description: Design tokens for tti-ux. Ships TTI as the default institutional theme; other TAMUS themes extend the base.

maroon.$description: Primary brand anchor. Sampled from tti-overview + factsheet; slightly darker than PPTX #5C0825, both are in TTI's accepted range.

maroon-deep.$description: High-contrast maroon from 508-accessible PPTX. Use for critical UI where AA contrast is non-negotiable.

gold.$description: Primary accent. TTI signature warm-ochre gold — not bright Aggie yellow.

navy.$description: Secondary accent. Used in option-1 PPTX and on infographic sections.

teal.$description: Tertiary / category color. Use sparingly, for data-viz category differentiation.

charcoal.$description: Body copy. Not pure black — softer, matches print collateral.

neutral.$description: Warm-neutral ramp. Intermediate stops at 150/250/450/550/650/750/850 are warm/sand-leaning so they harmonize with maroon over extended viewing; use them for row-stripe / hover / selected states on data-table surfaces. Lineage: shadcn's stone/zinc 12-stop ramps; hex values are tux-original.

semantic.$description: Base semantic colors for the tti theme. Split so restricted-tier (brand maroon) and something-broke (true red) stop visually competing. All four target WCAG 2.2 AAA on the surfaces they're consumed in; verified by /contrast-audit.

fill.$description: Saturated maroon panel fill — always dark enough for white text on top to pass AA. Use for filled maroon panels (footer, hero, page-header maroon tone).

text._description: text-secondary and text-muted both target WCAG 2.2 AAA (7:1) on white; hierarchy comes from weight/size/transform rather than color. Verified by /contrast-audit on 2026-04-27.

semantic.$description: tti theme semantic colors — same values as the base color.semantic block; restated here so each theme owns a full set.

focus._description: Two-ring focus token. Outer 2px maroon + inner 2px sand halo reads cleanly on dense surfaces. The sand inner is the tux-specific signature.

chart.$description: Categorical chart palette — eight series, qualitatively distinct under normal vision and across major colorblind types. Maroon at 1 keeps brand presence; the rest cycle through warm-neutral and earth-toned series.

map.$description: Map choropleth ramps — 5-step sequential (quantile-binned). Maroon for traffic-direct metrics; slate for non-traffic context layers. --map-outline is the state outline / district boundary hairline; --map-flow colors O-D arcs and dot-density dots.

tti-hc._description: High-contrast theme — WCAG AAA; drawn from the TTI 508-accessible PPTX. For users who opt in, and as our WCAG AAA audit reference.

primary.$description: HC maroon (maroon-deep), pinned literal — the 508-accessible PPTX anchor.

fill.$description: HC marketing panel — same darker maroon as the brand.

muted.$description: Clears AAA (7.5:1 on white). Flatter than the light theme's muted because HC is the accessibility-first variant.

rampOverrides._description: HC overrides of Nuxt UI's primary palette steps. Pinning both 400 and 500 to #500000 aligns Nuxt UI primitives with the HC brand maroon (ADR-0005). The 400 override isn't reached in HC (Nuxt UI's .dark path never fires) but is kept for ADR-0005's full-override discipline.

semantic._description: HC semantic colors all target AAA on white.

focus._description: Two-ring focus on HC — pure black outer + white inner at 5px total width. Bigger ring meets HC's wider-target conventions.

elevation._description: Elevation in HC — shadows nuked across the board; overlay swaps to a 2px border, pinned to a 1px hairline, so depth still reads at the contrast floor.

chart._description: HC chart palette — collapsed to high-contrast series. Charts in HC mode rely on patterns + labels as much as color.

map._description: HC map ramps — collapsed to a 5-step grayscale that clears AAA against both the page and itself. Real HC maps should layer hatching / dot patterns via consumer SVG fills.

tti-dark._description: Dark theme. Warm-charcoal surfaces (not pure black) so the editorial palette feels continuous with the light theme. Brand primary shifts from maroon to lifted TTI teal (#6BB4C0) on dark surfaces because lightened-maroon variants read pink against the small chrome elements where the accent lives (composer borders, focus rings, active-tab text). Maroon brand presence is preserved via `brand.fill` for marketing panels and via chart-1 pinned to wine so the primary chart series keeps the maroon association. Every semantic + chart + map value here targets AAA (7:1) on the dark page surface; numbers verified by /contrast-audit. See palette.md § Dark theme for the full rationale.

primary.$description: Lifted TTI teal (base {color.tti.teal} #4A8892 lifted for readability on dark). 7.1:1 on raised, ~8.2:1 on page, ~8.7:1 on sunken.

accent.$description: Gold accent unchanged on dark — its job is emphasis, not legibility.

fill.$description: Marketing panel fill — kept dark wine in all themes so white text on top passes contrast.

page.$description: Warm charcoal page — not pure black.

semantic._description: All target AAA (7:1) on the dark page surface #15100F. Lifted compared to the base semantic palette — sage → mint, gold → pale wheat, maroon → pale rose, teal → pale cyan.

rampOverrides._description: Override Nuxt UI's primary palette at the 400 step — the effective dark-mode button-fill knob (.dark resolves bg-primary → --color-maroon-400). Pinned to the same lifted teal as brand.primary so every primary-colored control in dark mode reads as TTI teal. Other maroon-* steps inherit from :root.

focus._description: Two-ring focus on dark — outer is the lifted-teal brand primary, inner is a deep warm-gold halo so the ring reads against both raised and sunken surfaces.

elevation._description: Elevation tiers on dark — same scheme; overlay alpha bumped so the sheet doesn't disappear into the warm-charcoal page.

chart._description: Categorical palette lifted ~15% to read against the warm-charcoal page. chart-1 is pinned to wine-rose rather than brand.primary so the primary chart series keeps the maroon brand association even though brand.primary resolves to teal in dark mode.

map._description: Choropleth ramps lifted for the warm-charcoal page. Maroon ramp reads as a warm gradient (deep maroon → light salmon); slate goes from near-charcoal up to near-white. Outline alpha boosted so state shapes stay visible on the dark page.

tamu._description: Texas A&M University — verified brand colors. Maroon 500000 is TAMU's canonical anchor.

pvamu._description: Prairie View A&M — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with PVAMU Marketing & Communications before releasing a PVAMU-branded build.

tarleton._description: Tarleton State — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with Tarleton brand team.

wtamu._description: West Texas A&M — PLACEHOLDER values. Confirm with WTAMU brand team.

ramps.$description: Nuxt UI 11-step color ramps. Defined as plain :root vars (not @theme) so Tailwind v4 doesn't tree-shake them — Nuxt UI's pre-compiled CSS references them but Tailwind only scans source templates.

maroon.$description: TTI maroon palette — anchored at #5C0025 = 500. Nuxt UI resolves color="primary" against these via app.config.ts primary: 'maroon'.

gold.$description: TTI gold palette — anchored at #DDAC37 = 500. Lightness curve keeps 50–200 readable on white and 700+ legible-as-text on white. Powers `warning` once app.config.ts flips warning: amber → gold.

globals.$description: Theme-independent design tokens emitted only on :root (the tti selector). Fonts, tracking, shadows, elevation tiers, radius, motion durations, easings, spacing, and the survey-rhythm density ramp.

font.$description: Four lane tokens (body / display / bold / elegant) per design/tux.md's four-family rule, plus the legacy --font-sans (Public Sans) retained for back-compat and --font-mono.

sans.$value: "Public Sans", "Franklin Gothic ATF", "Franklin Gothic", "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif

elevation.$description: Four-tier elevation language (flat / rest / hover / overlay / pinned). Lineage: Microsoft Fabric's elevation discipline.

motion.$description: Three-tier duration. fast = hover/focus; base = dropdown/accordion; slow = emphasis moments.

ease.$description: standard/emphasis/exit are the legacy back-compat aliases; survey/corridor/arrival are the transportation-tempo curves (Material 3 standard-effects family).

space.$description: Spacing utilities — supplement to Tailwind's spacing scale for places where a Tux component reaches into a CSS var directly.

rhythm.$description: Survey-rhythm density baseline — 4-8-12-16-24 ramp tuned for data-dense families. Lineage: Ant Design's 4/8/12/16/24 cadence.

breakpoint._description: Layout breakpoints. Values match Tailwind v4 defaults so `md:` / `lg:` utilities keep their intuitive meaning, but the SEMANTIC trigger for each breakpoint is documented here — what surface state changes at this width. Survey reference: Microsoft Windows UI kit responsive ladder (348/660/708/1020/1252), rebased to TUX's editorial-first content widths.

xs.$description: Mobile portrait. Single column, full-bleed cards, side rail (sidebar layout) is hidden behind a hamburger overlay.

sm.$description: Large phone / small tablet portrait. Still single column; touch targets enlarge, body type goes up half a step.

md.$description: Tablet. Two-column layouts viable; side rail still collapsed by default, accessible via hamburger.

lg.$description: Small laptop. Side rail expands to icon-only mode. Data-dense surfaces begin showing right-side context panels.

xl.$description: Standard laptop. Rail icons gain labels; editorial column hits its max-width (~72ch) with comfortable side margins.

2xl.$description: Wide desktop. Optional right-side context panel slot opens. Data-dense surfaces reach natural density.
